fairchild.label: Fairchild Semi
fairchild.description: The startup that started it all. Its alumni founded Intel, AMD, and Silicon Valley itself.

amd.label: AMD
amd.description: The persistent rival. They innovated in high-performance CPUs and challenged Intel\

nvidia.label: NVIDIA
nvidia.description: Started with gaming graphics, now the engine of AI. Their chips train the world\

arm_ltd.label: Arm Holdings
arm_ltd.description: They design the energy-efficient chip architecture found in almost every smartphone.

samsung.label: Samsung
samsung.description: A global electronics titan. They lead the world in memory chips, phones, and displays.

qualcomm.label: Qualcomm
qualcomm.description: The leader in mobile connectivity. Their tech connects your phone to 4G and 5G networks.

att.label: AT&T
att.description: The telecom monopoly (Ma Bell) that built the physical network infrastructure of the US.

spacex.label: SpaceX
spacex.description: Revolutionizing spaceflight with reusable rockets. Their goal is to make life multi-planetary.

ibm.label: IBM
ibm.description: Big Blue. They defined the mainframe era and helped launch the personal computer market.

tesla.label: Tesla
tesla.description: More than a car company. They made EVs desirable and are pushing robotics and autonomy.

dji.label: DJI
dji.description: The absolute leader in consumer drones. They made aerial photography accessible to all.

boston_dynamics.label: Boston Dynamics
boston_dynamics.description: Engineering dynamic robots that move with uncanny agility, mimicking humans and animals.

microsoft.label: Microsoft
microsoft.description: Software for the world. From Windows to Azure and OpenAI, they are a productivity giant.

oracle.label: Oracle
oracle.description: The database giant. Their software manages the critical data for the world\

salesforce_co.label: Salesforce
salesforce_co.description: The cloud pioneer. They replaced installed software with web-based business tools.

sap.label: SAP
sap.description: The backbone of global business. Their software manages operations for huge corporations.

meta.label: Meta
meta.description: Connecting people. They own Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, and are betting on VR.

tencent.label: Tencent
tencent.description: The giant behind WeChat. They dominate social media, gaming, and payments in China.

alibaba.label: Alibaba
alibaba.description: The e-commerce titan of China. They revolutionized digital retail and fintech.

bytedance.label: ByteDance
bytedance.description: The algorithm company. They built TikTok, the world\

uber_co.label: Uber
uber_co.description: Transportation on demand. They connected riders and drivers, creating the gig economy.

airbnb.label: Airbnb
airbnb.description: Hospitality by the people. They turned spare rooms into a global network of accommodations.

stripe_co.label: Stripe
stripe_co.description: Payment infrastructure for the internet. They handle the complexity of money for startups.

netscape_co.label: Netscape
netscape_co.description: The browser that mattered. They commercialized the web and sparked the dot-com boom.

spotify_co.label: Spotify
spotify_co.description: Streaming savior. They convinced the world to pay for music access instead of ownership.

netflix_co.label: Netflix
netflix_co.description: Changed TV. They moved from mailing DVDs to streaming, pioneering the binge-watching era.

deepmind.label: Google DeepMind
deepmind.description: Solved intelligence. Famous for AlphaGo, they use AI to solve fundamental scientific problems.

openai.label: OpenAI
openai.description: The lab behind ChatGPT. They pushed AI from research into a product used by millions.

anthropic.label: Anthropic
anthropic.description: AI safety first. Founded by former OpenAI members to build reliable, steerable AI.

huggingface.label: Hugging Face
huggingface.description: The GitHub of AI. An open community hub for sharing models, datasets, and demos.

sequoia.label: Sequoia
sequoia.description: The Midas touch. This VC firm backed Apple, Google, and NVIDIA when they were just ideas.

ycombinator.label: Y Combinator
ycombinator.description: The startup factory. Their accelerator launched Airbnb, Stripe, and Dropbox.

a16z.label: a16z
a16z.description: Software is eating the world. A modern VC firm that provides massive operational support.

xerox_parc.label: Xerox PARC
xerox_parc.description: The research lab that invented the mouse, windows, and ethernet, inspiring the modern PC.

asml.label: ASML
asml.description: The only company that makes the EUV machines needed to print the most advanced chips.

apple.label: Apple
apple.description: Design meets technology. They revolutionized computing with the Mac, iPod, and iPhone.

sony.label: Sony
sony.description: Changed entertainment with the Walkman. Now a leader in image sensors and gaming.

alan_turing.label: Alan Turing
alan_turing.description: The genius who cracked the Enigma code and conceived the theoretical basis of computers.

claude_shannon.label: Claude Shannon
claude_shannon.description: Father of Information Theory. He proved that all communication could be reduced to bits.

william_shockley.label: William Shockley
william_shockley.description: Co-inventor of the transistor. A brilliant physicist whose difficult management sparked the Valley.

dennis_ritchie.label: Dennis Ritchie
dennis_ritchie.description: The architect. He created the C language and Unix, the foundation of modern software.

tim_berners_lee.label: Tim Berners-Lee
tim_berners_lee.description: He gave the internet a face. He invented the World Wide Web, the browser, and HTML.

geoffrey_hinton.label: Geoffrey Hinton
geoffrey_hinton.description: The Godfather of AI. He believed in neural networks for decades until the world caught up.

yann_lecun.label: Yann LeCun
yann_lecun.description: The AI Scientist. He invented the convolutional networks that let computers

gordon_moore.label: Gordon Moore
gordon_moore.description: Predicted computing power would double every two years. His

steve_jobs.label: Steve Jobs
steve_jobs.description: The visionary who stood at the intersection of art and technology. He made gadgets personal.

bill_gates.label: Bill Gates
bill_gates.description: The coder who meant business. He realized software was the key and put Windows everywhere.

larry_ellison.label: Larry Ellison
larry_ellison.description: The samurai of software. He built an empire on enterprise databases and ruthless tactics.

morris_chang.label: Morris Chang
morris_chang.description: He revolutionized chips by creating the foundry model, building chips designed by others.

linus_torvalds.label: Linus Torvalds
linus_torvalds.description: He wrote Linux for fun. It now runs the cloud. He also gave us Git to manage code.

jeff_bezos.label: Jeff Bezos
jeff_bezos.description: He started selling books and ended up selling everything. He built the cloud (AWS) too.

jensen_huang.label: Jensen Huang
jensen_huang.description: The visionary who bet on GPUs. He turned a graphics company into the engine of AI.

larry_page.label: Larry Page
larry_page.description: He wanted to download the entire internet. He invented PageRank and co-founded Google.

sergey_brin.label: Sergey Brin
sergey_brin.description: The data scientist who co-founded Google. He helped organize the world\

reed_hastings.label: Reed Hastings
reed_hastings.description: He realized DVDs would die. He pivoted Netflix to streaming and changed TV forever.

elon_musk.label: Elon Musk
elon_musk.description: The real-life Tony Stark. He is electrifying transport and building rockets to Mars.

mark_zuckerberg.label: Mark Zuckerberg
mark_zuckerberg.description: He connected the world. He moved fast, broke things, and built a social empire.

chad_hurley.label: Chad Hurley
chad_hurley.description: He made video easy. Co-founded YouTube so anyone could broadcast themselves.

jack_ma.label: Jack Ma
jack_ma.description: The teacher who connected China to the world. He built an e-commerce giant from an apartment.

pony_ma.label: Pony Ma
pony_ma.description: The quiet architect of WeChat. He built a digital ecosystem that powers modern China.

travis_kalanick.label: Travis Kalanick
travis_kalanick.description: He hustled to build Uber. He changed urban transport with the push of a button.

patrick_collison.label: Patrick Collison
patrick_collison.description: He fixed payments. With Stripe, he made it simple for startups to accept money online.

satoshi_nakamoto.label: Satoshi Nakamoto
satoshi_nakamoto.description: The mysterious creator of Bitcoin. They solved the problem of digital trust without banks.

vitalik_buterin.label: Vitalik Buterin
vitalik_buterin.description: The boy genius behind Ethereum. He envisioned a blockchain that could run any program.

tim_cook.label: Tim Cook
tim_cook.description: The operations genius. He took Apple to a $3 trillion valuation by perfecting the supply chain.

sundar_pichai.label: Sundar Pichai
sundar_pichai.description: The product leader. He oversaw Chrome and Android before taking the helm at Google.

lisa_su.label: Lisa Su
lisa_su.description: The engineer who saved AMD. She executed one of the greatest turnarounds in tech history.

demis_hassabis.label: Demis Hassabis
demis_hassabis.description: The gamer neuroscientist. He founded DeepMind to solve intelligence and cure diseases.

sam_altman.label: Sam Altman
sam_altman.description: The operator. He scaled Y Combinator and then led OpenAI to release ChatGPT.

ilya_sutskever.label: Ilya Sutskever
ilya_sutskever.description: The brain behind GPT. He led the technical breakthroughs that made AI talk.

arthur_rock.label: Arthur Rock
arthur_rock.description: The financier. He helped start Intel and Apple, coining the term

don_valentine.label: Don Valentine
don_valentine.description: The grandfather of VC. His firm Sequoia backed the companies that defined the future.

masayoshi_son.label: Masayoshi Son
masayoshi_son.description: The risk-taker. His Vision Fund poured billions into the future of AI and connectivity.

marc_andreessen.label: Marc Andreessen
marc_andreessen.description: He built the first graphical browser. Now he funds the software eating the world.

peter_thiel.label: Peter Thiel
peter_thiel.description: The contrarian. He co-founded PayPal, backed Facebook first, and funds big ideas.

reid_hoffman.label: Reid Hoffman
reid_hoffman.description: The networker. He founded LinkedIn and is the

paul_graham.label: Paul Graham
paul_graham.description: The hacker philosopher. He created Y Combinator and wrote the playbook for startups.

max_levchin.label: Max Levchin
max_levchin.description: The coder behind PayPal. His work on fraud prevention proved humans are still useful.

roelof_botha.label: Roelof Botha
roelof_botha.description: The PayPal Mafia member who leads Sequoia. He spots the next big thing before anyone else.

turing_machine.label: Turing Machine
turing_machine.description: The theoretical blueprint for all computers. It defines the limits of what machines can calculate.

transistor.label: The Transistor
transistor.description: A tiny electronic switch. Billions of them pack onto chips to control the flow of electricity.

info_theory.label: Information Theory
info_theory.description: The math of the digital age. It defined

rsa.label: RSA Encryption
rsa.description: The digital lock and key system that keeps passwords and credit cards safe on the internet.

lithography.label: Lithography
lithography.description: A process using light to print incredibly tiny, complex circuit patterns onto silicon chips.

ic.label: Integrated Circuit
ic.description: Putting many transistors on a single chip. It solved the

ibm_360.label: IBM System/360
ibm_360.description: The mainframe that standardized software, allowing companies to upgrade hardware without rewriting code.

optical_fiber.label: Optical Fiber
optical_fiber.description: Glass strands that send data as pulses of light, forming the high-speed backbone of the internet.

microprocessor.label: Microprocessor
microprocessor.description: The entire brain of a computer compressed onto a single chip. It sparked the PC revolution.

floppy.label: Floppy Disk
floppy.description: The save icon in real life. Portable disks that moved software and files before the internet.

ethernet.label: Ethernet
ethernet.description: The standard cable system for connecting computers together in a local network (LAN).

x86.label: x86 Architecture
x86.description: The dominant language of PC processors. It has defined desktop computing for decades.

gps.label: GPS
gps.description: A satellite network that provides precise location and timing anywhere on Earth.

optical_disc.label: Optical Disc
optical_disc.description: A digital storage format using lasers (CD/DVD), revolutionizing music and software distribution.

tcp_ip.label: TCP/IP
tcp_ip.description: The common language of the internet that allows different networks to connect and talk.

arm_arch.label: ARM Architecture
arm_arch.description: A power-efficient chip design. It is the reason your smartphone battery lasts all day.

cisco_router.label: Cisco Router
cisco_router.description: The traffic cop of the internet. It directs data packets to ensure they reach the right destination.

foundry_model.label: Foundry Model
foundry_model.description: A business model separating chip design from manufacturing. It enabled the rise of NVIDIA and AMD.

nand.label: NAND Flash
nand.description: Storage that remembers data without power. It lives inside your USB drives, SSDs, and phone.

cdma.label: CDMA
cdma.description: A wireless technology that optimized radio signals, laying the groundwork for 3G networks.

gsm.label: GSM
gsm.description: The first global standard for digital mobile phones, enabling SMS and international roaming.

li_ion.label: Li-ion Battery
li_ion.description: High-energy rechargeable batteries. They make smartphones, laptops, and electric cars possible.

mp3.label: MP3
mp3.description: A way to compress audio files. It put a thousand songs in your pocket and disrupted music.

cmos.label: CMOS Sensor
cmos.description: The electronic eye. An affordable image sensor that turned every phone into a digital camera.

blue_led.label: Blue LED
blue_led.description: The breakthrough needed to create white LED light. It made energy-efficient screens possible.

bluetooth.label: Bluetooth
bluetooth.description: A wireless cord. It connects headphones, keyboards, and mice to devices over short distances.

ssl_tls.label: SSL/TLS
ssl_tls.description: The security protocol that puts the

wifi.label: WiFi
wifi.description: Wireless internet. It cut the ethernet cables and let us get online from the couch.

gpu_geforce.label: GPU (GeForce)
gpu_geforce.description: Originally for gaming graphics, now the heavy lifter for AI training and deep learning.

arm_cortex.label: ARM Cortex
arm_cortex.description: The specific processor cores found inside nearly every modern Android and iPhone.

lidar.label: LiDAR
lidar.description: Laser radar. It uses light pulses to create 3D maps, acting as the eyes for self-driving cars.

lte.label: LTE (4G)
lte.description: The 4th gen mobile network that brought true broadband speeds to phones, enabling video streaming.

alexnet.label: AlexNet
alexnet.description: The AI model that proved deep learning works, winning an image contest and starting the AI boom.

hbm.label: HBM
hbm.description: Stacked, ultra-fast memory. It feeds data to AI chips quickly enough to train massive models.

word2vec.label: Word2Vec
word2vec.description: A technique that teaches computers the meaning of words by analyzing their context.

gan.label: GAN
gan.description: Two AI networks competing against each other to generate realistic fake images.

tpu.label: Google TPU
tpu.description: A custom chip built by Google specifically to accelerate machine learning workloads.

transformer.label: Transformer
transformer.description: The architecture that pays attention to context. It is the secret sauce behind ChatGPT.

5g_nr.label: 5G (New Radio)
5g_nr.description: The latest mobile network offering massive speed and capacity for a hyper-connected world.

euv.label: EUV Lithography
euv.description: A manufacturing miracle using extreme UV light to print the world\

stable_diffusion.label: Stable Diffusion
stable_diffusion.description: An open AI that turns text into images, bringing art generation to personal computers.

nvidia_h100.label: NVIDIA H100
nvidia_h100.description: The engine of the AI revolution. A massive GPU designed specifically to train smart models.

unix.label: Unix
unix.description: An operating system philosophy of simplicity. It is the grandfather of Linux and macOS.

c_lang.label: C Language
c_lang.description: The building block of software. It allows code to run fast and close to the hardware.

sql.label: SQL
sql.description: The standard language for asking questions to databases and managing organized information.

oracle_db.label: Oracle Database
oracle_db.description: The gold standard for enterprise databases, powering the back-end of global business.

www.label: World Wide Web
www.description: The system of interlinked pages accessed via the internet. It made the net usable for everyone.

linux.label: Linux Kernel
linux.description: A free, open-source OS kernel that runs the internet, the cloud, and all Android phones.

python.label: Python
python.description: A readable programming language that became the primary tool for AI and data science.

windows_95.label: Windows 95
windows_95.description: The OS that introduced the Start menu and brought easy computing to the masses.

javascript.label: JavaScript
javascript.description: The language of the web browser. It makes websites interactive instead of just static text.

unreal.label: Unreal Engine
unreal.description: A powerful engine for high-fidelity 3D graphics, used in games and movie production.

bittorrent.label: BitTorrent
bittorrent.description: A decentralized way to share large files. It proved the power of peer-to-peer networks.

git.label: Git
git.description: A time machine for code. It helps developers track changes and collaborate on software.

unity.label: Unity
unity.description: A versatile platform for building games and 3D apps, democratizing game development.

aws_cloud.label: AWS Cloud
aws_cloud.description: Infrastructure as a Service. It lets anyone rent massive computing power over the internet.

ios.label: iOS
ios.description: The operating system that powers the iPhone, defining the modern mobile user experience.

android.label: Android
android.description: The open mobile OS that powers the vast majority of the world\

zero_trust.label: Zero Trust
zero_trust.description: A security model that assumes no user or device is safe, verifying every single request.

docker.label: Docker
docker.description: Packages software into shipping containers, so it runs the same on any machine.

walkman.label: Sony Walkman
walkman.description: It allowed people to carry their music with them, changing audio consumption forever.

macintosh.label: Macintosh
macintosh.description: The first mass-market computer with a mouse and graphical screen. It made computing friendly.

toshiba_t1100.label: Toshiba T1100
toshiba_t1100.description: The first commercially successful laptop. It established the clamshell shape we use today.

excel.label: Microsoft Excel
excel.description: The definitive spreadsheet. It replaced paper ledgers and became the language of business.

photoshop.label: Adobe Photoshop
photoshop.description: The verb for editing images. It set the standard for digital creativity.

mosaic.label: Mosaic Browser
mosaic.description: The first web browser to display images inline with text, sparking the internet boom.

netscape.label: Netscape Navigator
netscape.description: The browser that commercialized the web and fought Microsoft in the first browser war.

amazon_com.label: Amazon.com
amazon_com.description: Started as a bookstore, became the

netflix.label: Netflix
netflix.description: Shifted from DVD rentals to streaming, changing how the world watches TV.

google_search.label: Google Search
google_search.description: The engine that organized the world\

paypal.label: PayPal
paypal.description: A safe way to send money online. It spawned the

wikipedia.label: Wikipedia
wikipedia.description: A free encyclopedia written by volunteers. It democratized the world\

skype.label: Skype
skype.description: Popularized free video calls over the internet, making the world feel smaller.

linkedin.label: LinkedIn
linkedin.description: The social network for work. It moved the rolodex and resume online.

facebook.label: Facebook
facebook.description: Connected billions of people. It defined the social media era and the news feed.

youtube.label: YouTube
youtube.description: Broadcast Yourself. It democratized video creation and entertainment.

twitter.label: Twitter
twitter.description: The town square of the internet. A real-time feed of news and conversation.

iphone.label: iPhone
iphone.description: Combined a phone, iPod, and internet communicator. It put the internet in our pockets.

spotify.label: Spotify
spotify.description: Saved the music industry from piracy by offering legal, instant streaming access.

app_store.label: App Store
app_store.description: A centralized marketplace that made it safe and easy to install software on phones.

google_chrome.label: Google Chrome
google_chrome.description: A fast, minimalist browser that became the operating system for the modern web.

github.label: GitHub
github.description: Social coding. The home for open-source software and developer collaboration.

bitcoin.label: Bitcoin
bitcoin.description: Digital gold. The first decentralized currency based on blockchain technology.

uber_app.label: Uber App
uber_app.description: Push a button, get a ride. It pioneered the gig economy and on-demand transport.

stripe.label: Stripe
stripe.description: Seven lines of code that let any website accept payments. Infrastructure for the internet.

wechat.label: WeChat
wechat.description: The Chinese super-app. It combines messaging, social media, and payments in one.

zoom.label: Zoom
zoom.description: The video tool that just worked. It became the default for remote work and connection.

model_s.label: Tesla Model S
model_s.description: The car that proved EVs could be fast, beautiful, and have long range.

raspberry_pi.label: Raspberry Pi
raspberry_pi.description: A tiny, cheap computer designed to teach coding and power DIY electronics projects.

dji_phantom.label: DJI Phantom
dji_phantom.description: The ready-to-fly drone that popularized aerial photography for everyone.

slack.label: Slack
slack.description: Chat for work. It replaced internal email with channels and instant messaging.

ethereum.label: Ethereum
ethereum.description: A blockchain that runs code. It enabled smart contracts, DeFi, and NFTs.

alphago.label: AlphaGo
alphago.description: The AI that shocked the world by beating a human champion at the complex game of Go.

tiktok.label: TikTok
tiktok.description: Short-form video fed by an uncanny algorithm. It changed how we consume media.

oculus_rift.label: Oculus Rift
oculus_rift.description: The headset that kickstarted the modern consumer Virtual Reality market.

alphago_zero.label: AlphaGo Zero
alphago_zero.description: An AI that learned Go by playing against itself, without learning from human games.

starlink.label: Starlink
starlink.description: A constellation of satellites providing high-speed internet to remote corners of Earth.

fugaku.label: Fujitsu Fugaku
fugaku.description: The supercomputer that proved ARM chips could power the world\

copilot.label: GitHub Copilot
copilot.description: An AI pair programmer. It helps developers write code faster by suggesting completions.

chatgpt.label: ChatGPT
chatgpt.description: The chatbot that speaks like a human. It brought AI into the mainstream conversation.

midjourney.label: Midjourney
midjourney.description: An AI artist. It creates stunning, photorealistic images from simple text prompts.

perplexity.label: Perplexity
perplexity.description: An AI answer engine. It reads the web and gives you summaries with citations.

spacex_falcon9.label: Falcon 9
spacex_falcon9.description: The rocket that lands. It drastically lowered the cost of going to space.

starship.label: Starship
starship.description: A massive, fully reusable rocket designed to carry humanity to Mars.

claude.label: Claude
claude.description: An AI assistant built with safety in mind. It excels at reading long documents.

cursor.label: Cursor
cursor.description: A code editor built for the AI era. It integrates LLMs directly into the coding workflow.

sora.label: Sora
sora.description: An AI that dreams up realistic videos from simple text, understanding physics and motion.

postscript.label: PostScript
postscript.description: A page description language that sparked the desktop publishing revolution.

premiere_pro.label: Adobe Premiere Pro
premiere_pro.description: A timeline-based video editing software application.

pdf.label: PDF
pdf.description: Portable Document Format. The global standard for digital document exchange.

nano_banana.label: Nano Banana
nano_banana.description: The viral AI image model (Gemini 2.5 Flash) famous for its 3D figurine style and text rendering.
